rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Trial Date And Reopen Discovery
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR FOR MONDAY, APRIL 15, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 11:15 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant RPI Co.¿s motion to vacate trial date and reopen discovery: The court treated defendant¿s motion as a motion to continue the trial date, which is granted. The trial date is continued to April 14, 2014 at 11:15am in Department 503. The new trial date serves as the initial trial date for discovery purposes only as to RPI. The current trial date of July 8, 2013 serves as the initial trial date for discovery purposes as to all other defendants. No party opposed a trial continuance. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 11:15a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rpi Company¿S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Trial Date And Reopen Discovery
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR FOR MONDAY, APRIL 15, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 11:15 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant RPI Co.¿s motion to vacate trial date and reopen discovery: The court treated defendant¿s motion as a motion to continue the trial date, which is granted. The trial date, which has already passed and which has a current return date of April 15, 2013, is continued to February 10, 2014 at 11:15am in Department 503. The new trial date serves as the initial trial date for discovery purposes only as to RPI. Discovery remains closed as related to all other defendants. This case is severed from Brayton Group 610. No party opposed a trial continuance. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 11:15a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
